medical_history.chief_complaint: cough associated with thick gelatinous expectoration and dyspnea
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Symptoms started 6-8 hours prior to presentation. She had a history of congenital heart disease for which she had undergone Fontan procedure in her infancy. Prior to this episode, she had multiple similar episodes and was diagnosed with plastic bronchitis.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Congenital heart disease, Fontan procedure in infancy, plastic bronchitis

physical_examination.general_examination: Initially stable vitals, then developed hypoxia with oxygen saturation dropping to 90% requiring 100% FiO2
physical_examination.specialty_examination: On inspection, there was a large plaque like secretion completely obstructing the left main bronchus extending beyond the carina into the trachea.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Patient was found to be pregnant.

other_tests.findings: Large plaque-like secretion completely obstructing the left main bronchus extending beyond the carina into the trachea. A large bronchial cast was extracted from trachea and left main bronchus. The rest of the airways were patent.
other_tests.changes_over_time: Hypoxia improved after removal of casts but patient remained intubated for a period of 48 hours. On day 2, bronchoscopy was repeated to remove residual cast. The cast had recurred in the left main bronchus, and endobronchial N-acetylcystine was used as an adjunct to help remove the cast.

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient had a history of congenital heart disease and had undergone Fontan procedure in her infancy. She presented with cough associated with thick gelatinous expectoration and dyspnea, which are characteristic symptoms of plastic bronchitis. Bronchoscopy revealed a large plaque-like secretion obstructing the left main bronchus, confirming the diagnosis. The microscopic evaluation of the bronchial cast showed acellular material, and bronchial lavage was negative for infection, further supporting the diagnosis.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: intravenous boluses of 50 mcg, total of 175 mcg
large bronchial cast extracted from trachea and left main bronchus
used as an adjunct to help remove the cast
